website_recommendation.note: Present the current official late-2028 Artemis V Gateway/Lunar View mission as primary, and expose older provider-specific 2029/2030 public details only as an archived/reference layer.

current_official.title: Artemis V (current official mission)
current_official.summary: NASA's current 2026 materials describe Artemis V as a late-2028 lunar surface mission that integrates Lunar View with Gateway and completes the third crewed lunar surface expedition. Orion and Lunar View launch together on SLS Block 1B to lunar orbit, Orion delivers Lunar View to Gateway/HALO, a Human Landing System docks to Gateway, the surface crew descends to the lunar South Pole region, and the crew returns to Earth on Orion.
current_official.moon_base_context: NASA says Artemis V is when it expects to begin building its Moon base.
known: Current NASA sources target launch by late 2028
Current NASA Gateway reference material says Artemis V integrates Lunar View with Gateway
Current NASA Gateway reference material says Orion and Lunar View launch together as a co-manifested payload on SLS Block 1B to lunar orbit
Current NASA Gateway reference material says Orion delivers Lunar View to HALO, an HLS docks to Gateway, the crew descends to the surface, and the crew returns to Earth on Orion
NASA's 2023 Artemis V lander award materials publicly describe four astronauts with two descending to the lunar South Pole region for about a week

provider_context.default_copy: Use generic 'Human Landing System' in primary current-view copy.
provider_context.public_procurement_note: NASA selected Blue Origin in 2023 to develop the Artemis V sustainable lander, but current 2026 Artemis mission pages no longer use the older 2029 provider-specific framing as the primary mission summary.
provider_context.safe_website_treatment: Mention Blue Origin or Blue Moon only in a procurement / source note or archived/provider-specific view, not as the default hero statement.

unknown: No public exact Gateway arrival/departure state vectors
No public official orbital elements or point-by-point ephemeris for Artemis V
No public detailed phasing between Orion, Lunar View, Gateway, and HLS operations
No public exact Moon-base hardware manifest for the first Artemis V surface campaign

publicly_released_orbit.public_description: Current NASA pages describe Artemis V through Gateway in lunar orbit but do not publish official state vectors or precise orbital elements.

best_public_proxy.name: Southern 9:2 Gateway NRHO
rationale: Current official Artemis V public material still stages the mission through Gateway in lunar orbit.
NASA's Gateway FAQ provides public NRHO distance and period values.
NASA's NRHO white paper and NTRS records identify Gateway's operational orbit as a southern 9:2 resonant NRHO with about a 6.5-day period.
NASA hosts a public sample NRHO trajectory that is the best available stand-in for website visualization.
best_public_proxy.use_on_website: Appropriate for current and archived Artemis V orbit detail views, but only when explicitly labeled as a proxy / derived Gateway NRHO, never as exact mission ephemeris.

surface_mobility_and_base_context.base_building_note: NASA says Artemis V is when it expects to begin building its Moon base.
surface_mobility_and_base_context.ltv_current_status: NASA intends to begin using the Lunar Terrain Vehicle (LTV) for crewed operations during Artemis V.
surface_mobility_and_base_context.ltv_testing_note: NASA's rover Ground Test Unit work is meant to evaluate concepts for use beginning with Artemis V.
what_not_to_claim: Do not claim a final Moon-base layout is public.
Do not imply a pressurized rover or habitat module is confirmed for Artemis V unless treated only as broader campaign context.

phases.label: Launch
phases.summary: Orion and ESA's Lunar View launch together on SLS toward lunar orbit.

phases.label: Translunar transfer
phases.summary: The co-manifested stack departs Earth for the Moon.

phases.label: Gateway approach
phases.summary: Orion arrives in the Gateway staging orbit family near the Moon. NASA has not publicly released the exact operational ephemeris.

phases.label: Deliver Lunar View
phases.summary: Orion delivers Lunar View to Gateway, where it docks to HALO.

phases.label: Lander docks to Gateway
phases.summary: A Human Landing System docks to Gateway to receive the surface crew.

phases.label: Crew transfer and descent
phases.summary: Two astronauts transfer to the lander and descend toward the lunar South Pole region.

phases.label: Surface mission
phases.summary: About a week of surface exploration near the South Pole, with Artemis V also expected to begin the first Moon-base buildup and the first crewed use of the Lunar Terrain Vehicle.

phases.label: Ascent and redock
phases.summary: The surface crew returns to Gateway and transfers back to Orion.

phases.label: Return to Earth
phases.summary: The full crew departs lunar orbit and returns to Earth on Orion.

archived_provider_specific_profile.title: Artemis V (archived / provider-specific detailed public profile)
archived_provider_specific_profile.summary: Older NASA procurement material and ESA pages provide a more detailed Artemis V sequence tied to Blue Moon / 2029 or Lunar View / 2030 language. Those sources still show the same Gateway/NRHO architecture but include older schedule assumptions and step-by-step operational detail that should not override the current late-2028 NASA framing.

gateway_staging_orbit.name: Southern 9:2 Gateway NRHO
notes: Same Gateway staging-orbit family as the current official view
Chosen for Earth communications, eclipse avoidance, and South Pole access

phases.label: Launch
phases.summary: Launch from Kennedy Space Center with Orion and Lunar View co-manifested.

phases.label: Low-Earth orbit checks
phases.summary: Mission control and crew complete final checks in low Earth orbit.

phases.label: Translunar injection
phases.summary: The upper stage sends Orion and Lunar View toward the Moon.

phases.label: Transposition and extraction
phases.summary: Orion separates, turns around, docks with Lunar View, and extracts it from the upper stage.

phases.label: Outbound cruise
phases.summary: Orion and Lunar View coast toward the Moon.

phases.label: Lunar flyby / gravity assist
phases.summary: The detailed ESA public profile includes a lunar flyby / gravity-assist step before Gateway arrival.

phases.label: NRHO arrival
phases.summary: Arrival at Gateway's near-rectilinear halo orbit.

phases.label: Dock with Gateway
phases.summary: Orion docks with Gateway and connects / installs Lunar View.

phases.label: Transfer and activation
phases.summary: Crew and cargo transfer to Gateway, Lunar View is activated, and the lander is prepared.

phases.label: Surface departure
phases.summary: Two astronauts undock in the lander for a South Pole surface sortie.

phases.label: Surface / orbital split operations
phases.summary: Two astronauts work on the surface while Orion remains docked to Gateway for about six days in NRHO.

phases.label: Surface ascent and redock
phases.summary: The lander lifts off from the Moon and redocks with Gateway.

phases.label: Prepare for Earth return
phases.summary: Crew and samples transfer back for the Earth-return leg.

phases.label: Departure from NRHO
phases.summary: Orion departs Gateway's NRHO at the optimal Earth-return point.

phases.label: Return flyby and cruise
phases.summary: The detailed public profile includes a lunar gravity-assist burn and homeward cruise.

phases.label: Reentry and splashdown
phases.summary: Crew module separates, reenters Earth's atmosphere, and splashes down in the Pacific.

orbit_proxies.same_underlying_proxy_note: Both current and archived Artemis V views use the same southern 9:2 Gateway NRHO family in public sources; the duplicated files are intentionally the same geometry with different labels and UI use-cases.

source_drift_notes: Current 2026 NASA Artemis pages present Artemis V as a late-2028 lunar surface mission, with the Gateway reference page still explicitly describing Lunar View delivery to Gateway and a surface sortie via HLS.

Older NASA procurement material publicly ties Artemis V to Blue Origin's Blue Moon lander and a 2029 demonstration schedule; this is still valid procurement context but no longer the best current mission-summary framing.
ESA's Lunar View page explicitly says it will be updated to reflect the latest Artemis architecture and still carries older schedule/detail assumptions.
Because current and archived public materials both use Gateway/NRHO, the website should distinguish them by freshness and level of detail rather than by implying different orbit families.
copy_constraints: Do not claim NASA has published exact Artemis V orbital elements or ephemeris.
Do not mix 2029/2030 dates from older Blue Moon / ESA materials into the default current hero view.
Use generic 'Human Landing System' in default current-view copy unless the UI is explicitly presenting procurement context.
Always label NRHO orbit data as proxy / derived when used in the UI.
Because current and archived views both use Gateway NRHO, label the secondary view as archived / provider-specific / detailed, not as a different current architecture.
Use 'expected' / 'intends' wording for Moon-base and LTV statements.
